senses.definition: a toy or device designed to create loud or distinctive sounds when activated, often used for celebration, entertainment, or signaling purposes during events and parties

examples.x: The children grabbed their noisemakers and ran outside to join the parade.
Everyone blew their noisemakers when the clock struck midnight on New Year’s Eve.
The noisemaker made such a loud sound that it startled the dog.
She handed out noisemakers to all the guests at the party before the countdown began.

senses.definition: a small-scale replica or simplified version of a real musical instrument, often made of plastic or other child-safe materials, designed for play and introduction to music and sound exploration for childre

examples.x: The kids enjoyed playing together with their toy instruments at the park.
At the daycare, the children gathered around the toy instruments, making their own little band.
She got a toy instrument set for Christmas and spent the whole day trying each one.
The toddler loved banging on the toy instrument, creating her own little tunes.

senses.definition: a small handheld toy that produces a "moo" sound when tipped or turned over, imitating the sound of a cow, providing amusement and fun for children
a small toy that makes a squeaking sound when squeezed or pressed, often used for play and entertainment for pets and children alike

a handheld toy for infants, typically consisting of a handle with a hollow ball or container containing small objects, such as beads, that create rattling sounds when shaken, designed to engage and stimulate babies' senses

examples.x: The sound of the baby rattle caught the puppy’s attention.
The soft sound of the baby rattle calmed him down after his nap.
I bought a new baby rattle as a gift for the newborn.
I packed a baby rattle in the diaper bag for the trip to the park.
She loves to grab her baby rattle and make it shake.

senses.definition: a small, handheld device used to produce a high-pitched sound when blown into, often used for communication, signaling, or as a toy for play and fun

examples.x: The referee blew the whistle to stop the game after a foul was committed.
She carried a whistle with her while hiking in case of emergencies.
The lifeguard used a whistle to alert swimmers of the approaching storm.
He always keeps a whistle handy when jogging in the park for safety reasons.

senses.definition: a musical toy made of a corrugated tube with beads or small objects inside, producing unique whirring and vibrating sounds when spun or shaken, providing a mesmerizing auditory and visual experience
a small, noise-making device used in celebrations and parties, producing a loud noise when blown into, adding to the festive atmosphere and fun
a mechanical toy that resembles a monkey, featuring cymbals that it clangs together when wound up, creating an amusing and entertaining display, commonly used as a novelty and collectible item

a traditional Japanese toy made from bamboo and paper, shaped like a clapper, and when swung back and forth, it creates a clacking sound, often used by children during summer festivals or as a noisemaker
a traditional Jewish noisemaker used during Purim celebrations to make noise and drown out the name of the villainous character Haman

a novelty toy consisting of a long, flexible tube with a small horn on one end, and when the tube is compressed or stretched, it produces a comical groaning or moaning sound, providing amusement and entertainment

examples.x: The children laughed when they played with the groan tube, making funny sounds during the party.
I picked up a groan tube at the toy store for the kids' birthday, knowing they would love the noise it made.
I found the groan tube in the toy chest and decided to show it to my friends at the gathering.
The groan tube added an extra layer of fun to the sound effects at the Halloween party.
My younger brother spent hours with his groan tube, fascinated by how it made such a funny noise.

senses.definition: a classic toy that features a surprise element, with a figure, often a clown or puppet, hidden inside a box, and when the crank is turned, the figure pops up with a "jack-in-the-box" sound, providing delight and surprise for children

examples.x: The children laughed in surprise when the jack-in-the-box popped open.
I found an old jack-in-the-box at the flea market and decided to buy it.
My little sister loves playing with her colorful jack-in-the-box.
He gave the jack-in-the-box a twist and waited for the surprise to happen.
For his birthday, I gave him a classic jack-in-the-box as a nostalgic gift.

senses.definition: a musical toy that resembles drumsticks and features built-in electronic sensors that produce drum sounds when tapped on different surfaces
